sections.content:
Major changes happened! Let me analyze:
1. The red shape on the right side disappeared (white border turned to black)
2. The red block on the left (rows 30-33, cols 12-17) disappeared (turned to dark gray)
3. A new red 8x6 block appeared at rows 24-29, cols 18-21 (on the left side)
4. The blue shape on the right also disappeared (white border turned to black)
So clicking on the right-side red shape caused:
- The red shape template on the right to be removed
- The red piece on the left to be removed
- A new red block appeared somewhere on the left
This seems to be a matching game! Clicking a shape on the right "uses up" the corresponding colored piece from the left and places a new block. Let me look at the current state more carefully.

        print(f"{i:2d}: {line}")

print()
print("=== RIGHT PANEL (rows 10-53, cols 34-63) ===")
for i in range(10, 54):
    line = ascii_lines[i][34:64]
    if any(c != 'B' for c in line):
        print(f"{i:2d}: {line}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see what happened:
1. The right-side shapes lost their white borders - they're now just solid colored shapes (red and blue)
2. The red block from the left (rows 30-33, cols 12-17) disappeared
3. A new red 6x4 block appeared at rows 24-29, cols 18-21
4. The gray+red+dark red horizontal strip on the left was split - the red part is gone, leaving gray and dark red separated
So clicking on the red shape on the right "consumed" the red piece from the left and placed a new red block somewhere. The white borders on the right shapes were removed.
Wait, let me reconsider. The right-side shapes now have NO white borders - they're just solid colored shapes. The white borders were removed. So the mechanic seems to be:
- Clicking a shape on the right removes its white border
- It also consumes the matching colored piece from the left side
- A new block appears somewhere
Actually, looking more carefully at the left side:
- The original red piece (rows 30-33, cols 12-17) is now gone (turned to 'c')
- A new red block appeared at rows 24-29, cols 18-21
- The gray blocks that were next to the red are still there but now separated
So the game seems to be about clicking the right-side shapes to "solve" them, which consumes corresponding left-side pieces. The goal might be to click all the right-side shapes.
Let me also check: the right side now has two solid shapes without borders:
1. Red shape (rows 17-21)
2. Blue shape (rows 34-38)
Let me click on the blue shape on the right to see what happens.

print("Changed cells:", changed)
